title: Attiveri Bird Sanctuary
tagline: Attiveri Bird Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Attiveri Bird Sanctuary is a village in the Mundgod taluk of Uttara Kannada district in Karnataka.
overview.description: Attiveri Bird Sanctuary is a village in the Mundgod taluk of Uttara Kannada district in Karnataka. It is located 15 km away from Mundgod. Spread over an area of about 2.23 km2 (0.86 sq mi), the sanctuary is located in and around the Attiveri reservoir. The part of the sanctuary surrounding the reservoir has riverine and deciduous forests.

topPlaces.description: Agadi (Mundgod) is a village in the southern state of Karnataka in India. It is located in the Mundgod taluk and it is under kawar districts in Uttara Kannada.

topPlaces.name: Rato Dratsang
topPlaces.description: Rato Dratsang (Dratsang in Tibetan means 'monastery', lit. "monk's nest"), also known as Rato Monastery (sometimes spelled Ratö Monastery), Rato Dratsang is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery of the Gelug or "Yellow Hat" order.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Attiveri Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Attiveri Bird Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Attiveri Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (32 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (31 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Attiveri Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Attiveri Bird Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Attiveri Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Attiveri Bird Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Attiveri Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bendalgatti, Nelliharvi, Agadi, Uttara Kannada, Bammigatti, Rato Dratsang.
